subject: Mozi Minute: The Gift-Review Loop
body:
Words I like: People help people before they help businesses.

Mozi Minute: The Gift-Review Loop

Most businesses make the mistake of having owners ask for reviews directly. But people don't feel connected to owners - they feel connected to the employees who helped them. Here's how to use that psychology to get more authentic reviews.

Have your employees surprise customers with an unexpected gift or extra service. Then, as the owner, send a personal message: "Hey [name], [employee] told me how much they enjoyed [serving] you. They get a [$50] bonus if you leave them a 5-star review sharing your experience. Would you mind taking 2 minutes to help them out?"

Why this works:

- Unexpected gifts create reciprocity
- Customer feels they're helping the employee, not the business
- The request comes after value is already given
- Clear, specific ask with a concrete benefit to someone they know

As promised, money in under a minute.

Want more real world tactics that drive revenue? We just opened spots for our next Scaling Workshop in Vegas on April 7/8th.

Help them help you,

Alex

PS - Your employee's face when they realize being extra generous just earned them another bonus...
